message:
fix: correct cancellation propagation in transactions, sessions, and buffer

Four related fixes addressing a class of bug where cancellation signals
were either lost or caused incorrect state transitions:

1. database/mysql.rs — MySqlTransaction::commit and ::rollback
   previously set self.finished = true unconditionally before awaiting
   the actual SQL command.  If the command was cancelled (e.g., by a
   timeout or scope drop), the transaction was marked finished even
   though the commit/rollback never executed on the wire, leaving the
   connection in an indeterminate state.  Fix: only set finished = true
   inside the Outcome::Ok branch.  Adds tests verifying that a
   cancelled commit correctly leaves needs_rollback set.

2. database/postgres.rs — Identical fix applied to PgTransaction.
   Same deferred-finished pattern with matching cancellation tests.

3. session.rs — The map_send_error helper was mapping all SendError
   variants to SessionError::Disconnected, masking SendError::Cancelled
   as a disconnection.  Fix: extracts a map_send_error helper that
   propagates Cancelled as SessionError::Cancelled.  Two new tests
   verify correct propagation through send, choose_left, and
   choose_right.

4. service/buffer.rs — Buffer::close did not wake tasks parked in
   poll_ready, so they would remain suspended indefinitely after the
   buffer was closed.  Fix: drains and wakes all parked poll_ready
   waiters on close.  New test verifies that parked waiters observe
   the closed state immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
